scenarios.name: Upside Breakout
scenarios.description: Volatility compression resolves to the upside with volume confirmation

scenarios.name: False Breakout / Whipsaw
scenarios.description: Price tests extremes but fails to sustain

risk_flags: LLM_RISK: Potential false breakout or reversal due to ADX indicating a weak trend.

hypotheses.statement: Volatility breakout signals with volume > 2x average produce higher Sharpe than baseline
hypotheses.test: Compare Sharpe of vol_breakout with volume_surge_threshold=2.0 vs 1.5

hypotheses.statement: Requiring 2 confirmation candles reduces false breakouts without excessive lag
hypotheses.test: Compare win_rate and expectancy with confirmation_candles=1 vs 2 vs 3

hypotheses.statement: During an upside breakout regime, the volatility_breakout strategy will perform better when using a shorter look-back period (LBP) of 20-30 days compared to the standard LBP of 50 days.
hypotheses.test: Backtest both strategies on historical data with a sample size of at least 100 instances of upside breakouts. Compare their cumulative returns and Sharpe ratios over a 3-month holding period.

hypotheses.statement: The mean_reversion strategy will be more effective in downside breakout scenarios when using a stop-loss threshold of 5-7% compared to the standard threshold of 3%.
hypotheses.test: Backtest both strategies on historical data with a sample size of at least 50 instances of downside breakouts. Compare their cumulative returns and drawdowns over a 2-month holding period.

hypotheses.statement: The trend_following strategy will exhibit better performance in false breakout/whipsaw scenarios when using a moving average with a longer period of 100-150 days compared to the standard period of 50 days.
hypotheses.test: Backtest both strategies on historical data with a sample size of at least 80 instances of false breakouts/whipsaws. Compare their cumulative returns and maximum drawdowns over a 4-month holding period.
